title: Large-print + high-contrast variants of all four core families
context: Low-vision learners and learners with visual processing differences need manipulatives with larger numerals and higher contrast than the standard variant. The project's style guide already mandates large-print and high-contrast as required variants, but the generator engine's core implementation does not yet specialise these beyond scaling. A dedicated implementation is needed that applies accessibility-specific rules: minimum numeral size, maximum contrast (black on white or white on black), and thicker cut guides.
objective: Implement large-print and high-contrast generator variants for all four core manipulative families, reviewed and signed off by an accessibility reviewer.
acceptanceCriteria: Large-print variant increases numeral/label size to at least 24pt equivalent at the printed physical size; all other dimensions scale proportionally from the spec.
High-contrast variant uses only black (#000000) and white (#FFFFFF); no other colours; numerals and cut guides have sufficient stroke weight to be legible on a standard office printer.
Both variants pass the automated verification harness (dimensions and relationships unchanged from spec).
Both variants pass accessibility sub-checks: contrast ratio > 7:1 (WCAG AAA) for the high-contrast variant; colour-blind simulation confirms no information loss.
Variants are generated automatically by the generator engine when the spec includes 'large-print' or 'high-contrast' in its colour-mode list.
Reviewed and signed off by an accessibility reviewer; sign-off recorded.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-style-001.json -- style guide v1 (large-print and high-contrast requirements)
output: Large-print and high-contrast generator variants integrated into the engine, with example output PDFs for all four families, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
